title: phemeranthus teretifolius
intro: Phemeranthus teretifolius, also known as five-stamen rock-jasmine or five-stamen fivespur, is a rare, annual succulent plant native to the southwestern United States. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The health benefits of P. teretifolius include reducing inflammation, treating wounds, and providing antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of P. teretifolius can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, although its use is not widely documented and further research is needed to fully understand its potential benefits and applications.
